title: Shinjis Internal Psycho Analysis Leliel or Yui sama
display_title: Shinjis Internal Psycho Analysis Leliel or Yui sama
wikitext:
[]: Shinji's Internal Psycho-Analysis-Leliel or Yui-sama?             Quote Post by BEsERk EVA01 » Thu Feb 07, 2008 5:09 am During the phase when Eva 01 and Shinji were in the Dirac Sea of Leliel, Shinji had these strange, psychological thoughts. Considering that only Yui-sama and Leliel were in physical contact with each other, was it Leliel trying to penetrate Shinji's thoughts, or was it that Yui-sama was trying to contact Shinji, or was it actually just Shinji going crazy?

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Thu Feb 07, 2008 5:16 am SEELE thinks Leliel was trying to make contact with Shinji. With Areal and Armi doing similar stuff later in the series their theory makes sense.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Feb 07, 2008 5:45 am From a thread on ANF: http://www.tesuji.org/anf/thread_182820.html Shin-seiki wrote: I recall that in an interview with Tsurumaki that was posted recently, he indicated that the idea in #16 was that they wanted to depict communication between Shinji and the Angel, but thought it would be hokey to have the Angel speaking with a corny, cliche, "TAKE ME TO YOUR LEADER!" type voice, so they decided to portray the interaction as an internal dialog that Shinji is having with an 'other self'. This is consistent with the theme of all the psychic interactions of the Angels with the pilots (Shinji in #16; Asuka, in #22; and Rei in #23), which is that the Angels function as a mirror held up to the pilots in which their innermost secrets, which they have surpressed and hidden away, are revealed...

[]:             Quote Post by BEsERk EVA01 » Thu Feb 07, 2008 6:14 am Awesome! Thanks for the info, Ornette; that practically answered all my questions. EDIT: Then someone should edit the info on the FGP...in case some newbies ask the same question...because the paragraph on this in the FGP is outdated...

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Feb 07, 2008 7:32 am BEsERk EVA01 wrote: EDIT: Then someone should edit the info on the FGP...in case some newbies ask the same question...because the paragraph on this in the FGP is outdated...

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Thu Feb 07, 2008 8:42 am Wait wait wait, second Shinji was angel? That makes no sense, I always (well, nearly always) took Tsurumaki's words literally. Communitacting with Angel idea was scrapped and Shinji really was communicating with himself there. KT - The first draft of the scenario was actually a dialog between Shinji and the Angel. However, we felt it would be too anti-climactic to have an Angel start talking like some pulp fiction alien (speaks while tapping his Adam's apple with his hand) "Your analog mode of thought is incorrect." So we came up with the idea actually used in this episode, which was to have Shinji converse with himself. I guess I might've been wrong here, but Angel-Communicating here just plain sucks and doesn't make any sense

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Thu Feb 07, 2008 11:14 am Xard wrote: I guess I might've been wrong here, but Angel-Communicating here just plain sucks and doesn't make any sense

[]:             Quote Post by Themaninblack » Thu Feb 07, 2008 11:33 am BobBQ wrote: Xard wrote: I guess I might've been wrong here, but Angel-Communicating here just plain sucks and doesn't make any sense

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Feb 07, 2008 12:18 pm If Shinji 2 is Leliel, why would it say it's Shinji Ikari?

[]:             Quote Post by Themaninblack » Thu Feb 07, 2008 12:55 pm Reichu wrote: If Shinji 2 is Leliel, why would it say it's Shinji Ikari ?

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Thu Feb 07, 2008 2:30 pm No no no guys, you don't get it. Angel mindrape attacks in 22 and 23 doesn't really have that much in common with ep 16. Just look Ep 16 and right after either one of those and you'll see it. Notice how these two angels are clearly attackers/outsiders in the mental landscape. Just look how Asuka addresses the angel during the mindrape, same with Rei. Now compare this to all the stuff that's going on in Ep16 mindfuck. Kaworu can't be compared to previous angel attempts at communicating Reichu wrote: If Shinji 2 is Leliel, why would it say it's Shinji Ikari?

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Thu Feb 07, 2008 9:00 pm Themaninblack wrote: BECAUSE ALL THE ANGELS ARe IN HIS MIND!

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Thu Feb 07, 2008 9:34 pm Themaninblack wrote: BECAUSE ALL THE ANGELS ARe IN HIS MIND! NEW FAN WANK YEAH!!!!

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Thu Feb 07, 2008 9:37 pm NAveryW wrote: New? No. Matt Greenfield actually briefly mentioned the possibility that everything in NGE is just in Shinji's mind and represents the struggles of adolescence, but says that, in the end, it doesn't matter.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Thu Feb 07, 2008 10:49 pm sauce, delicious sauce! finally a creator-interview weighing in on "WTF is going on in these scenes?"

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Feb 07, 2008 11:36 pm @Xard: The two questions that he answers about episode 16, first he says that it was just going to be another Angel attack, then as more of the continuity was written about the Angels, it got turned into one where an Angel takes interest in a human being. With that, as he answers the second question, I can't interpret it any other way except: Seeing as how this episode was going to be able Leliel's interesting in Shinji, instead of the Angel talking directly with Shinji, we had Shinji talk to another version of himself. Seeing as how his first statement still holds, where does "we decided to insert an episode where an Angel appeared to take an interest in humans." fit in with the second statement? Well, it would either be Shinji talking to himself or hallucinating it all, which is a complete non-sequitur, or he's actually somehow communicating with an Angel through this interaction. The similarity to 22 and 23 is: Asuka talks to another version of herself, Rei talks to another version of herself. The the latter's case, she actually identifies that this other self she's talking to is Armisael.

[]:             Quote Post by AsukaChan » Fri Feb 08, 2008 1:10 am I was really thinking about this with the RP and all. XD But I wondered.. Could Leliel have said it was Shinji because it knew he probably wasn't going to question (or would accept it much more readily) due to his situation? That perhaps it wouldn't be as unsettling to him than if he knew an Angel was looking into his mind? I could be reaching a bit, but it just doesn't make sense to me that Shinji would just up and start thinking those things to himself, even if he believed he was going to die. That would imply that he was aware of his own issues, and that just doesn't add up for me. I thought the entire purpose (or, rather, the underlying message) of it all was that Shinji didn't get it, or understand himself? In the episodes with Asuka and Rei, Arael and Armisael seemed to bring out the thoughts/experiences in them that they wouldn't have thought about normally. Arael's first inclination was to dive into Asuka's deepest memories and feelings. But Arael and Armisael were both more advanced and seemed like they were crafted for the very purpose of infiltrating the mind. So perhaps that was Leliel's way of prodding Shinji?

[]:             Quote Post by Themaninblack » Fri Feb 08, 2008 1:28 am NAveryW wrote: Themaninblack wrote: BECAUSE ALL THE ANGELS ARe IN HIS MIND! NEW FAN WANK YEAH!!!!

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Fri Feb 08, 2008 1:32 am Themaninblack wrote: Holy Shit I was joking. Is he really that dumb?

[]:             Quote Post by Themaninblack » Fri Feb 08, 2008 1:38 am Sachiel_13 wrote: Themaninblack wrote: Holy Shit I was joking. Is he really that dumb?
